Sunday scaries are the REAL DEAL.

Do you have a go to spot you like to clean before Monday morning approaches? 

#sundayscaries #cleanhomehappyhome #getorganized #cleaningmotivation

If a vacuum isn’t up your friends or family’s alley, check out our full gift guide (link in bio!) for 24 other AMAZING ideas. 

#holidayshopping #holidaygiftguide #cleaningexpert

I’ve always been into hand and nail care, and using products that keep my nails healthy and strong is super important to me. With the cold weather upon us here in 🇨🇦, I notice that my nails can become weak and brittle if I’m not treating them well. 

Did you know that lavender and tea tree essential oils might be able to help?

I’d love to hear if you’ve tried this tip before. Let me know in the comments below 💅🏼 

#nailcaretips #expertadvice #essentialoils

Is your jewelry looking a little dull or lacklustre lately? If so, I’ve got a quick and easy tip to get it sparkling again.

Take 1/2 cup of rubbing alcohol and pour it into a bowl with a small squirt of dish soap. Put your gold or silver jewelry into the solution and give it a gentle stir. Let it sit for a few minutes and then give it a GOOD rinse with warm water!

**As with any cleaning product you try for the first time, you want to use an abundance of caution to be sure you won’t damage your stuff! Rubbing alcohol should only be used on solid gold, silver and stones.  Never on costume jewelry or pearls. If you’re unsure if your jewelry can withstand the alcoholic stick to mild dish soap and water only**

#expertadvice #jewelrycleaning #diycleaning

With so many cleaning products available, it’s often difficult to decide which ones you need to buy and which ones you can safely (and effectively!) make on your own. 

I want to share with you 3 DIY recipes that I make all of the time, and use to clean 80% of my home with. Check out our latest video (link in bio!) to learn more. 

#diy #cleaningexpert #expertadvice #cleanhomecleanmind

I used to recommend vinegar, but it doesn’t compare to the results you get with fabric rinse. Clothes come out cleaner, towels don’t stink, and the rinse also cleans your washer while it works on clothes. You’ve got to try it to see how it works for yourself! #cleanmyspace #downy #laundry @downy - not sponsored just a big fan.

This cooler is also a gorgeous side table. Comment TABLE and I’ll send you the link. If you still have that moldy cooler you bring out for backyard gatherings this is a nice summer upgrade that is built to last. It’s made from a stone resin and can withstand extreme temperatures so you don’t need to bring it in during the off season (mine has sat outside all Canadian winter long). I’ve had this for over a year now and I’m totally obsessed. It holds over 50 cans and is super solid!

FOLLOW for more great cleaning and home finds and ideas #cleanmyspace

Can you imagine? Like to never have a stain you can’t conquer again? It’s a goooooood feeling.
#laundry #stains #stainremoval #cleanmyspace cleaningexpert

We have ALL had days that make us feel crummy for one reason or another, and the to top it off, we come home to a bunch of stuff that needs cleaning, folding or our attention and it can feel like it never ends. I want to remind you that even if you did something *really* small today, go ahead and celebrate that. You unloaded your dishwasher this am? Amazing! You folded some clothes that were on your bed? Perfect. You emptied the trash? Yeah!!! I know these seem small, but on days that feel overwhelming these can feel like a lot of work to expend mental and physical energy on and celebrating that tiny win will make you feel better. And you know what? WHY NOT? Why not compliment yourself on something when your day felt hard?

👉 Follow for more cleaning advice, tips and motivation #cleanmyspace
Thanks for having me @dyson ! I want a Hush Jet Mini for golf season!! #dyson #cleanmyspace

I know the loop: I have to clean, I don’t want to clean, but if I don’t my house will be messier. I feel stressed. UGH. Yeah I know that loop very well. But I want to give you your confidence in cleaning and help you take control back.

👉Comment WAVE and I’ll send you my (seriously) game-changing cleaning system. 
👉Follow for more cleaning tips, tricks and hacks.

#cleaning #cleanmyspace #cleaningexpert

Teaching kids to help around the house and getting them to be willingly participate is H A R D! I’ve done this with my 8 year old since she was very young and she’s a pretty helpful kid and doesn’t resist chores. I’ve lead with ‘I’m going to teach you life skills that will set you up for success’ instead of ‘you need to earn your keep around here’. Everyone parents differently, but I think this approach has worked so well for me and my daughter because she sees it as me imbuing her with a responsibility rather than foisting it upon her because she is somehow indebted to me. Subtle, but makes a big difference.  Anyway, this formula works every time and my daughter helps with kitchen work and laundry regularly. Save for when you need to teach your kid(s)! 👉Follow for more cleaning tips, tricks and hacks 
#cleanmyspace #cleaningexpert

Cleaning, chores, housework, domestic labor, jt all gets in the way of a relationship if you don’t know how to talk about it. And in my almost 2 decades of running a cleaning company and talking to thousands of clients, and in my 15 years of being on YouTube and talking to hundreds of thousands of you, AND my extensive years in therapy (lol fr), I’ve learned there is just one exact thing you need to say. And you come from a place of ME, not YOU when you say it:

“A clean house matters to me. And you matter to me. And I know I matter to you. When our house isn’t clean, and I don’t get any help, yes I’m frustrated on the surface but underneath it makes me feel like my needs don’t matter to you.”

THEN STOP TALKING. Hush. Let that sink in for your partner.

What happened here is that rather than blaming, shaming, or nagging, you just came from a place of “you”. It’s not about making them feel bad, it’s about telling them your needs are not being met in the partnership. And that hits much different.

In my experience, this is the most successful path forward when there’s struggles around cleaning or housekeeping or domestic work.

Would you try this? Or do you have any conversations that have worked on this topic? Weigh in!

Follow for more cleaning tips, advice and techniques!

#cleanmyspace

If you’re new here, or even if you’re not, here’s a little intro about me and a story about how I became an accidental cleaning expert. It’s not because I love cleaning, it’s because I hate cleaning!
